dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3037 IS: No AliPay Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Scott of Florida (for himself, Mr. Tuberville, and Mr. Justice) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To prohibit United States persons from engaging in financial transactions with AliPay.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No AliPay Act of 2025.

subsection.text: A United States person may not conduct a financial transaction with AliPay (China) Internet Technology Company Limited (referred to in this Act as AliPay), including by using any application or payment processing service operated by AliPay.

subparagraph.text: a transaction that in any way or degree affects interstate or foreign commerce—

clause.text: involving the movement of funds by wire or other means; or
involving one or more monetary instruments; or

subparagraph.text: a transaction involving the use of a financial institution that is engaged in, or the activities of which affect, interstate or foreign commerce in any way or degree.

paragraph.text: The term United States person means—

subparagraph.text: a national of the United States;
an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence;
an entity organized under the laws of the United States or under the laws of any jurisdiction within the United States (including a foreign branch of such an entity); or
an individual who is physically present in the United States.
